global.openToWorkText: Open to Work · UK & EU Remote

home.title: Lewis Hadden
home.name: Lewis Hadden
home.tagline: Senior full stack engineer based in Peterborough, UK. Nine years shipping React, Next.js & Azure at IBM, ADP, ERGO. Currently specialising in modernising platforms that refuse to be simple.

about.title: About
about.label: Get to know me
about.description:
Hi, I’m Lewis — a senior full stack engineer based in Peterborough, UK, with nine years of shipping production software across automotive, insurance, payroll, and government sectors.

I’ve led engineering teams at IBM, Sopra Steria, and ERGO, architected cloud infrastructure on Azure and AWS, and delivered everything from React SPAs and Next.js platforms to serverless backends and real-time data pipelines — always with a focus on code that’s fast, secure, and built to last.

Currently contracted to ADP on their flagship RUN payroll platform. I’m open to my next senior or lead role — remote, UK or EU — whether freelance or permanent.

highlights.title: React & Next.js
highlights.sub: Enterprise SPAs and platforms shipped for IBM, ADP & ERGO

highlights.title: Cloud Architect
highlights.sub: Azure (Cosmos DB, Functions, Event Hubs) & AWS (Lambda, EC2, S3)

highlights.title: Team Lead
highlights.sub: Led engineering teams at IBM, Sopra Steria & ERGO

highlights.title: Secure & Compliant
highlights.sub: Payroll, insurance, sanctions & GDPR delivered at scale

items.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer (Contractor)
items.description: Building enterprise features for ADP's payroll platform, used by 950,000+ clients worldwide. Focused on React migrations, WCAG accessibility compliance, and controlled feature rollouts via feature flags.

items.title: Lead Full Stack Engineer
items.description: Led full-stack delivery of production digital services for a travel insurance MGA. Defined company-wide dev standards, architected Azure infrastructure, and established the foundational UI boilerplate and local development tooling.

items.title: Cloud Infrastructure Architect
items.description: Sole architect of a greenfield Azure cloud infrastructure for UK Power Networks. Designed the database schema, security model, and produced comprehensive architecture documentation for a new energy sector microsite.

items.title: Lead Software Engineer
items.description: Led development of real-time traffic management dashboards for the Singapore Land Transport Authority. Delivered complex Angular components and engineered a bespoke split-tunnel VPN solution for secure environment access.

items.title: Senior Full Stack Developer
items.description: Led an agile team delivering a new e-commerce journey for Audi UK. Architected the full AWS stack — Lambda, DynamoDB, EC2, API Gateway — while managing sprints, presenting to stakeholders, and mentoring engineers.

items.title: Lead Front End Developer
items.description: Led a frontend team building a drag-and-drop micro-frontend platform for Audi UK, enabling content editors to create bespoke SPAs without writing code. Pioneered a shared Redux store across independent React apps and achieved 100% test coverage.

items.title: Front End Developer
items.description: Contributed to the Audi UK website in a fast-paced agile team. Built React/Redux features, wrote automated test suites with WebdriverIO and BrowserStack, and helped migrate the legacy tech stack to AWS.

items.title: QA Tester
items.description: Performed manual and automated testing across IBM web applications. Wrote automated test suites using WebdriverIO and Selenium with BrowserStack cross-browser coverage, and managed defect escalation through JIRA.

items.title: Smiley Pets
items.description: Smiley Pets is a professional pet care website I designed and built from scratch for a local business established in 2010. The site showcases their dog walking and pet sitting services, highlights their credentials — fully insured, DBS checked, and Canine First Aid certified — and builds trust through client testimonials and a photo gallery of happy pets in their care. Key features include a pet photo film strip, a full gallery, a contact and booking section, and social media integration. Built with Next.js, React, TypeScript and Tailwind CSS, and deployed on Vercel.

items.title: Sip Happens
items.description: Sip Happens is a passion project I designed and built from scratch — a dedicated review platform for espresso martini enthusiasts. The site features honest, detailed bar reviews from around the world, a blog exploring espresso martini culture, and an interactive global map that lets users explore reviewed bars by location. Visitors can submit bar recommendations to help grow the platform's coverage. Built entirely with Next.js, React, TypeScript and Tailwind CSS, the platform uses a PostgreSQL database to store and serve reviews, blog posts, and submission data. The site is deployed and live at sip-happens.co.uk, with an active social presence on Instagram and TikTok.

items.title: ADP RUN
items.description: I am currently contracted to ADP, a global leader in payroll and HR solutions, where I am working on their ADP RUN platform. My role involves developing new features and enhancements for the platform, using feature flags to enable a highly customisable user experience. I use React and Tailwind CSS to build a highly responsive, mobile-first user experience. I am also responsible for writing comprehensive unit tests using Jest and React Testing Library, as well as e2e tests using Cypress to ensure the reliability and maintainability of the codebase.

items.title: Sanctions Checker
items.description: I developed a sanctions checking tool for an insurance MGA which allows them to search for a name against the public database of open sanctions to determine whether or not a user is eligible for travel insurance. The tool allows them to view all of the publicly available data associated with a sanctioned person. We tested various sanction checking tools, for example OFSI - HM Treasury and the government UK Sanctions List, and found the accuracy of my solution to be far superior to all of the other tools we tested against. The tool consists of 2 parts on the backend, a daily job which queries the latest dataset and stores them in a CosmosDB instance, and an API that performs a search against the database given a name as input. The front-end is a Next.js application that provides a search input field and interfaces with the API to show the set of results. I built the tool to perform a thorough search by consolidating several SQL queries to check against multiple search vectors such as aliases and abbreviated first names to ensure that no sanctions are missed.

items.title: Oralieve
items.description: I contracted for Oralieve to complete several tasks on their Shopify store. I was responsible for implementing a new patient samples form, which allows dental professionals to request free samples of Oralieve products for their patients. I also implemented a minimum spend free shipping feature, as well as many other new features and bugfixes.

items.title: AirDoctor Webhook
items.description: AirDoctor is a platform that enables its users to connect with nearby doctors whilst abroad, making it easy to claim back on their travel insurance policy to recieve the treatment they need. Whilst working at ERGO Travel Insurance Services, I architected, developed and deployed the entire Webhook that ingests and processes insurance claims from the AirDoctor platform. This has enabled ERGO to underwrite the sales of new insurance policies for their partner insurance companies through the AirDoctor platform, bringing in a large stream of new revenue. I was the sole developer working on the project, and drove it from it's conceptualization, all the way through to delivery of the enterprise-grade, production ready solution. Adhering strictly to best practice secure programming principals in both code and infrastructure, the final solution is extremely robust and secure. I also built a docker stack with local azure emulators to enable local development, mitigating the risks of performing any unwanted actions on an environment. This docker stack can be used for all development team projects going forward, providing an extra layer of security as well as offline development capability.

items.title: Audi Form Builder
items.description: I led a development team in creating a new advanced technical capability. This capability allowed content editors to create custom forms on the Audi website without writing new code. They can drag and drop any of our React micro-frontends onto a new page, and the system will work seamlessly. I was responsible for designing and implementing the solution while leading the development team throughout the project. Currently, the Audi site has four forms created using the Audi Form Builder.

items.title: Cookie Control
items.description: I was responsible for single-handedly designing and implementing the Cookie Control system for the Audi UK website to ensure compliance with GDPR regulations. To achieve this, I utilized Adobe Launch to develop custom rule sets and JavaScript tags, allowing me to control the triggering of third-party tracking tags and custom analytics based on the users' cookie settings.

items.title: Audi Digital Transformation
items.description: During my five years on the Audi UK project, I was pivotal in modernizing and transforming their technology infrastructure. This involved migrating their legacy tech stack onto AWS and completely overhauling their website. As a front-end development team leader, I spearheaded the development and implementation of cutting-edge web apps and new SPAs. Additionally, I contributed to the technical innovation of their new infrastructure before transitioning into full-stack development. I gained a great deal of knowledge and experience during this project, and it remains one of my most cherished professional accomplishments.

items.title: Traffic Management Portal
items.description: I created bespoke real-time traffic monitoring, reporting, and management dashboards for the Singapore Land Traffic Authority (LTA). These dashboards provided LTA with an online portal to view live updates on their road traffic data. The dashboards were user-friendly, intuitive, and graphical, allowing LTA to monitor road incidents, create custom automated alerts, view live congestion maps, statistical charts and graphs, and much more. As a result, this solution became one of the most highly-capable and feature-rich land traffic management solutions ever created. I was responsible for developing complex Angular components for the dashboards, migrating legacy code, and reviewing and deploying new code across environments.

items.title: Audex
items.description: I architected and developed the front-end foundations for a significant module powering Audex, an online media visualisation and analysis portal. Renowned as a highly-skilled React developer, I was brought on board to single-handedly work on the React Front-End for the platforms' Analyse module. I began by developing a POC, successfully demonstrating the ability to display a PowerBI graph in a React app which subscribes to the PowerBI data source and renders real-time analysis data. After delivering on my React competency, I concluded my time on the project after creating the official repository for the Analyse module and the boilerplate containing the underlying technology to power the entire module.

items.title: Home Greening Microsite
items.description: I was solely responsible for the architecture and design of the entire cloud infrastructure for a proposed new micro-site for UK Power Networks. I led the cloud and Front-End development teams during the design and planning phases, guiding them through the solution and implementation. The client preferred Azure as their cloud provider, and I worked closely with them to ensure that my infrastructure design complied with their strict security standards. After collaborating with the client's security team, I created an optimal cloud infrastructure solution that met their functional and security requirements. I analysed the functionality of the microsite to gather the data requirements and used this to devise the most efficient database schema for Cosmos DB. I also created detailed architecture diagrams and a comprehensive technical design breakdown of the Azure architecture, including justifications and decisions made for each service, along with implementation and configuration steps.
